name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: Day 54 Tuesday. US 10Y 4.25% (Monday close, +0.09 bps 1D) — FLAT, BOND MARKET NOT CONFIRMING INFLATION RE-ACCELERATION DESPITE BRENT +5%. US 2Y 3.60% (-0.10 bps 1D) — flat, Fed path unchanged. The bond-market complacency IS a signal: Treasury market pricing Wednesday April 22 ceasefire renewal as base case + Q1 earnings 13.2% YoY fundamental floor + Fed April 28-29 hold (JPM: 34% probability of ZERO 2026 cuts, up from 10% pre-war). Real yields NOT falling enough to make gold attractive = dual headwind for non-yielding bullion (Gold -0.7% Mon). Powell succession term expires May 15, 2026 (~3 weeks) — Trump nominee selection will be a major market event independent of crisis. India 10Y GoI ~6.90-6.95% band (~50% crisis-linked: oil-CPI pass-through + FII debt outflows); state auction Rs 16,900 cr April 21 expected in 6.85-7.00%. If Wednesday lapses + Brent $100+: US 10Y may re-rate to 4.35-4.45% on oil-inflation transmission; if renews: drifts to 4.15-4.20% on dovish pricing.

signals.content: 10Y yield topped 4.06% as surging oil prices raised inflation concerns. Typical safe-haven bond rally did NOT materialize.
Yield rose to 4.37%, highest since July 2025. Market removed every rate cut, pricing in possible hike. 'Pandemonium' in global bond markets.

US 10Y at 4.44% (+10.5% monthly). Oil-driven inflation expectations rising (~40bps in March). Fed trapped in stagflation -- can't cut (inflation), can't hike (growth risk). 2Y at 3.61% = 83bps positive slope, but both elevated = stagflation pricing. India 10Y at 6.93% (+6bps) reflecting parallel pressure. ECB froze rate cuts March 19. All three major central banks (Fed, ECB, RBI) on hold simultaneously -- global monetary policy paralysis.

US 10Y dropped to 4.34% (-2.2%) -- first meaningful yield decline in weeks. 'End war' headlines drove some flight-to-safety unwind. But yields dropping while oil stays elevated is UNUSUAL -- signals market shifting from 'inflation' to 'growth/recession' concerns. Stagflation trap: yields can't go much higher (growth risk) or lower (inflation risk). Short-end (2Y at 3.60%) barely moved -- curve dynamics. India 10Y GOI at 7.00% (+7bps). CONFIRMED.

US 10Y eased to 4.31% (-0.7%). Pezeshkian peace signal created modest flight-from-safety (yields should RISE when safe-haven demand drops, but they FELL -- indicating the market shifted to pricing growth/recession concerns over inflation). DXY dropped below 100 to 99.79. Short-end (2Y at 3.60%) barely moved -- curve dynamics suggest long-end pricing growth deterioration while short-end stays anchored on Fed policy. Eurozone inflation surged to 2.5% -- energy +4.9% from -3.1%. ECB rate hike speculation emerging. Global stagflation pricing intensifying. India 10Y at 7.03% -- breached 7% on record FY27 borrowing. (Sources: yfinance, TradingEconomics, Eurostat)

10Y yield FELL (-2bps to 4.30%) on second consecutive peace signal day -- UNUSUAL for second session running. Expected: peace reduces safe-haven demand, yields should rise. Actual: yields fell. Long end falling while short end stable (2Y at 3.61%) = curve pricing GROWTH DETERIORATION, not inflation relief. Bond market has shifted from 'inflation worry' to 'recession worry.' This is the most important divergence in the macro landscape -- equities celebrating de-escalation while bonds pricing recession.

ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022, 19.3-point surge in 2 months). 17 of 18 industries reported higher costs. Employment STILL in contraction despite manufacturing expansion. This is textbook STAGFLATION -- can't cut (inflation) and can't hike (growth risk). Market pricing 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). The ISM data validates the bond market's recession signal over the equity market's de-escalation euphoria.

US 10Y at 4.30% -- falling despite oil surge and VIX spike. This is the OPPOSITE of risk-on: reduced safe-haven demand normally pushes yields UP. Long end pricing growth deterioration while short end (2Y 3.61%) anchored on Fed policy. The yield curve behavior confirms stagflation: inflation persists (ISM Prices 78.3) but growth prospects deteriorate (employment contraction). When bonds and equities disagree, bonds are usually right. (Sources: yfinance, TradingEconomics)

US 10Y at 4.31% (-0.1% 1D). The bond market is shifting from inflation worry to recession worry -- yields falling despite oil at $109 suggests growth concerns are overtaking inflation fears. April 10 CPI is the next binary event. ISM Prices at 78.3 (highest since June 2022). If CPI hot, yields reverse higher and gold faces real-yield competition. 2Y at 3.61%, 2-10 spread at 70bps. The spread compression from 83bps to 70bps in one week signals the long end is leading the move lower -- growth deterioration pricing accelerating. India 10Y GOI at 7.11-7.13% with RBI MPC starting today (April 6). Gold futures opened at $4,783 -- bonds and gold agree (recession/haven), equities disagree (pricing peace). S&P futures -0.28% -- mild, not panic.

US 10Y yield at 4.34% (+0.5% 1D, +4.6% 1M). Real yield competition constraining gold at 4.34%. March NFP +178K vs +60K expected -- strong jobs but ISM Employment contracting = late-cycle divergence. JPM March CPI forecast: 3.4% YoY. April 10 CPI print will test whether yields break higher on inflation or fall on recession fears. US 2Y at 3.62%. Yield curve dynamics complex: strong jobs supports higher yields, but ISM contraction and war risk pull toward lower yields. DXY above 100 (100.12) supported by yield differential. (Sources: yfinance, FinancialJuice)

US 10Y at 4.34% (+0.5% 1D, +4.6% 1M, +3.7% 3M) -- backing up on risk-on bid. US 2Y at 3.62%. The yield curve is reflecting (a) higher inflation expectations from oil shock, (b) Fed hike probability rising on March CPI forecast (3.4% headline per JPM), (c) deficit/war financing concerns, (d) DXY strength on 100.08. The 10Y had been compressed by safe-haven flows earlier in March; now unwinding as the dollar-strength paradox reasserts. Pre-deadline positioning: yield could spike further if deadline passes and oil hits $130+.

EVENING UPDATE: US 10Y at 4.34% (+0.5% 1D). Gold cooling from $4,796 to $4,686 reflects rising real yield competition. Late-cycle divergence: NFP strong (+178K), ISM employment contracting. 2Y at 3.62% (+0.4%). Spread 72bps. April 10 CPI will determine if hawkish pricing intensifies. Market pricing zero Fed cuts and 52% hike probability. (Sources: yfinance, Bloomberg)

US 10Y BARELY MOVED ON CEASEFIRE -- 4.34% (+0.2%) [CONFIRMED]. The bond market's reaction to the ceasefire was remarkably muted. US 10Y at 4.34% (+0.2% or +1 bp). US 2Y at 3.62% (-0.2% or -1 bp). The flat 10Y on the day Brent crashed -13.5% and oil-driven inflation pressure dramatically eased is the cleanest tell that the bond market doesn't yet price a big Fed dovish shift. The Fed remains in 'patient hold' mode. Hot CPI Friday is now less likely to force a hike but doesn't immediately force a cut either. The 2Y dovish nudge of -1 bp signals modest revision of front-end Fed expectations. DXY -1.1% -- dollar weakness is the bigger market reaction than rates. Watch for whether the curve steepens further if Goldman/Moody's revise recession probabilities lower.

US 10Y yield barely moved on ceasefire: -5bps to 4.29% on April 8 despite Brent crashing -11.5% and S&P +2.5%. MOST IMPORTANT NON-MOVER of the day. Expected: significant drop (10-15bps) on oil crash + de-escalation. Got: marginal. Bond market doesn't believe ceasefire changes the Fed's trajectory. March CPI (expected 3.2-3.4% on Friday April 10) already baked in. Cumulative inflation damage from 6 weeks of $100+ oil can't be reversed by fragile 14-day ceasefire. When bonds and equities diverge on the same news, bonds are usually right. US 2Y -1bp to 3.60%. DXY -1.1% to 98.58 -- dollar weakness larger than rates move. (Source: yfinance, Trading Economics, CNBC)

US 10Y rose modestly to 4.32% (+0.6% / ~+2.7bps from 4.29%) on CPI day [CONFIRMED, Federal Reserve H.15]. REMARKABLY MUTED for a headline miss (3.3% vs 3.4% consensus). Expected +3-5bps, got ~+2.7bps. Bond market already priced inflation persistence from 6 weeks of $100+ oil. 2Y yield closed at 3.59% (+0.1%) [CONFIRMED, yfinance]. Stagflation trap: can't sell bonds on inflation (growth risk too high), can't buy bonds on growth fears (inflation too high). Bond-equity divergence PERSISTS: equities pricing peace + rate cuts, bonds pricing inflation persistence + growth risk. When they disagree, bonds usually right.

4.32% Friday. US CPI 3.3% with hot +0.9% monthly -- biggest since June 2022. Fed hike probability >50%. Expected to rise to 4.35-4.45% at US Monday open on Islamabad failure + blockade continuation. Bonds being validated over equities -- inflation persistence confirmed, peace that equities priced didn't arrive.

US 10Y at 4.30% (-2bps from 4.32%) -- BARELY MOVED despite Trump 'Iran reached out' claim driving S&P +1.0% and oil reversal below $100. Expected rise to 4.35-4.45% on Islamabad failure/blockade, or significant fall on deal hope. Got neither -- -2bps, essentially flat. Bond market is 'the adult in the room,' refusing to commit to either 'deal happening' or 'escalation' narrative. Caught between opposing forces: deal hopes reduce inflation expectations (yields down) vs stagflation trap persists (can't sell on inflation, can't buy on growth). Bond-equity divergence at WIDEST of entire war. S&P at 6,886 (erased ALL war losses) says 'resolution imminent.' 10Y at 4.30% says 'we'll see.' US 2Y at 3.60% (+0.3%). India 10Y GOI ~6.90% (+5bps from April 13) on FII debt outflows and oil-driven inflation. (Sources: markets.md, yfinance, Federal Reserve H.15)

4.26% (-4bps from 4.30%) — THE ADULT IN THE ROOM. Essentially UNCHANGED. The most important NON-MOVER. Expected rise on Islamabad failure/blockade OR significant fall on deal hope. Got: neither. Bond market caught between deal hopes (reduce inflation) and stagflation trap (oil elevated, CPI 3.3%). These forces cancel. Bond-equity divergence at WIDEST of the entire war: S&P at 6,967 erased ALL war losses says 'deal imminent, crisis over'; 10Y at 4.26% says 'we'll see.' If bonds start MOVING — either direction — that's the regime signal. 4.40%+ = inflation winning, Fed hikes. 4.10% = recession winning, equities sell off on growth fear. Currently: paralysis. (Sources: Treasury data, yfinance)

EVENING UPDATE: 10Y STILL at 4.26% — BOND PARALYSIS CONFIRMED. Through the full trading day, 10Y moved less than 1bp despite S&P hitting new post-war high (6,967, 35 points from ATH). Bond-equity divergence now at WIDEST of the entire war and WIDENING: S&P says 'crisis over, deal imminent'; 10Y says 'show me.' PPI data confirmed oil is the ONLY inflation driver (core PPI +0.1% MoM) — if oil normalizes, inflation drops immediately. But bonds don't believe oil will normalize with active blockade. The paralysis regime: 4.40%+ = inflation winning (Fed hikes, equities crash); 4.10% = recession winning (equities sell on growth fear). Currently frozen between these outcomes. US 2Y at 3.61%, spread 65bps. India 10Y GOI at 6.97% (+4bps) on FII debt outflows — diverging from US pattern. (Sources: Treasury data, yfinance, BLS PPI)

Day 52 Sunday. Friday Apr 17 close 4.25% (-1.5% 1D, -0.3% 1M); US 2Y 3.60%. The -1.5% on a no-Fed-news day is meaningful: Brent crashed -9% wire on Araghchi Hormuz-open → energy-inflation tail-risk reduces → implied Fed rate-cut path marginally more dovish. This was the FIRST session of the war in which bonds AND equities CONVERGED on the same crisis-resolution signal (S&P record +1.2%, 10Y -1.5%). Powell Thursday April 16 'patience' framing — no April or June cut priced now. MONDAY DIAGNOSTIC: if Monday Brent +5-8% (per markets.md probability distribution centered on +5-8%), US 10Y yield +5-8bps inflation re-pricing expected. The bond-equity convergence re-diverges if Iranian reciprocal measures fire. India 10Y GOI ~6.97% (+12-18bps since crisis start); FPIs pulled ₹17,689cr from FAR G-Secs since crisis; FII debt outflows compounding the India-US divergence pattern. Bond-paralysis regime (4.10% recession / 4.40%+ inflation) broke on Friday toward dovish; Monday reverses if oil rallies. (Sources: yfinance ^TNX, markets.md C3 + D, intel.md)

MONDAY GAP UP 5-8BPS EXPECTED ON BRENT REVERSAL [REPORTED]. Friday's -1.5% (to 4.25%): Brent crash re-priced oil-inflation tails; Fed cut probability at June FOMC moved from ~35% to ~48% (CME FedWatch); 10Y rallied from 4.31% intraday to 4.25% close on dovish re-read. Monday gap: Brent Globex $98 = oil-inflation tail re-engages -> Fed cut probability back down to ~35% -> 10Y sells off. Expected: 4.30-4.33%; gap UP ~5-8bps. Bond market is fastest-mover — Monday open is the first tradeable re-price. Pairs with gold's regime-shift read: if 10Y gaps UP and gold ALSO gaps UP, the central-bank accumulation thesis is the dominant marginal driver.

US 10Y 4.25% FRIDAY (-1.5% 1D) — FED APRIL 28-29 FOMC IN 8 DAYS; STATEMENT FRAMING 'TEMPORARY ENERGY SHOCK' VS 'INFLATION PERSISTENCE' IS LIVE [REPORTED]. CME FedWatch 99.3% probability no rate change at April 28-29 FOMC. Front-month oil futures +50% over period with longer-dated futures much smaller rise → expectations of temporary-shock (per Fed minutes). Brent $95 Monday tests whether "temporary" framing survives March CPI +0.9% m/m (core +0.2%). Tape risk: If any Fed speaker (Waller, Williams, Daly) walks back June-cut probability Monday-Tuesday, yields spike across curve independent of crisis.

US 10Y YIELD 4.29% (+1.0% 1D) — THE MECHANISM BEHIND GOLD'S OVERNIGHT BREAKDOWN [CONFIRMED, yfinance]. The 10-year Treasury yield rose from 4.25% Tuesday toward 4.29% during the overnight (01:26-01:30 IST) period when the Vance-cancellation/talks-collapse narrative peaked and Brent spiked to $101.97. The yield rise was the vehicle through which gold broke down to $4,677.22 at 01:30 IST: oil-inflation fears → bond selloff → higher real yields competed with gold → gold sold as funding currency while DXY bid absorbed the safe-haven flow. After Trump's extension announcement, Brent retraced, the inflation fear moderated, 10Y backed off, and gold recovered most of the overnight loss to $4,780. The 10Y at 4.29% remains within the 4.25-4.35% range that has held since the Touska seizure; 1M is -2.3% reflecting the broader steady-state of contained-crisis pricing on the long end. The regime diagnostic is clean: this crisis is being priced as inflation event (10Y sensitive to oil) not financial-system event (10Y would gap higher on bank stress, which hasn't happened).

edges.context: Bond-equity divergence at WIDEST of the war. 10Y barely moved (-2bps to 4.30%) while S&P erased ALL war losses. Bond market refuses to commit to deal or escalation narrative. Stagflation trap intact.

Bond-equity divergence WIDEST of the war: S&P +1.0% to 6,886 (ALL war losses erased), 10Y flat at 4.30%. Equities pricing 'deal within days,' bonds saying 'not sure.' When they disagree, bonds usually right.
Higher US yields -> capital flows to US -> away from EM including India. India 10Y GOI ~6.90% (+5bps Apr 13). FII debt outflows + oil inflation pressure.

trigger_points.mechanism: 5% was the level that crashed markets in Oct 2023. Would signal inflation expectations unanchored. Massive equity selloff risk. Currently at 4.30% -- 70bps away.
